---
title: "PEX26-F51L Research | Regina Ring & Yehuda Dinaii"
subtitle: "Computational and structural analysis of a peroxisomal disease variant"
date: last-modified
date-format: "MMMM D, YYYY"
title-block-style: none
---

## Overview

**Peroxisome biogenesis factor 26 (PEX26)** is a tail-anchored peroxisomal
membrane protein that recruits and anchors the hexameric AAA-ATPase complex
PEX1–PEX6 to the cytosolic face of the peroxisome. The complex uses ATP
hydrolysis to retrotranslocate the import receptor PEX5 back to the cytosol,
enabling repeated rounds of peroxisomal matrix protein import. In yeast, Pex15
is the functional homologue of human PEX26.

The homozygous missense variant **F51L** in **PEX26** is associated with
peroxisome biogenesis disorders on the Zellweger spectrum, and has been linked
to sensorineural hearing loss. It was first described in 2019 in four siblings
from an Ashkenazi Jewish family [[Tanaka et al.
(2019)](https://molecularcasestudies.cshlp.org/content/5/1/a003483.long)]. To
our knowledge, only six individuals worldwide are known to carry this mutation:
the four siblings reported in that study, and [our two
daughters](about_and_contact.html).

::: {.column-margin}
<div style="color: #333;">

### 💬 PeroxiBot (NotebookLM)

Chat with an AI trained on the PEX26-F51L literature.

[Open PeroxiBot](https://notebooklm.google.com/notebook/6c5550cb-f1e7-475b-89b1-9422fa615f96/preview){target="_blank"}

---

*Last updated: {{< meta date >}}*

</div>
:::

## Key Questions

- How does F51L alter the structure and dynamics of the PEX26 cytosolic domain?
- Does the mutation affect recruitment or binding of the PEX1/PEX6 complex?
- How is this related to sensorineural hearing loss?

## What We Do

We use [AlphaFold 3](https://github.com/google-deepmind/alphafold3/tree/main) for
structure prediction and [CHARMM-GUI](https://www.charmm-gui.org/) for the peroxisome membrane
to build accurate structural models of both the wild-type and the **PEX26-F51L** variant.

<figure>
<video width="100%" controls>
  <source src="movie.mp4" type="video/mp4">
</video>
<figcaption><strong style="color: #6464a0;">Movie 1:</strong> The PEX1/PEX6/PEX26 complex docked to the peroxisome membrane, generated by <a href="https://github.com/google-deepmind/alphafold3/tree/main">AlphaFold 3</a> and <a href="https://www.charmm-gui.org/">CHARMM-GUI</a>. For an interactive view with Mol* explore the structure <a href="structure.html">here</a>.</figcaption>
</figure>

We modeled the PEX1/PEX6/PEX26 double ring D1-D2 domains with a portion of PEX5
inserted in the central pore.

<figure>
<video width="100%" controls>
  <source src="movie_pex5_light.mp4" type="video/mp4">
</video>
<figcaption><strong style="color: #6464a0;">Movie 2:</strong> The PEX1/PEX6/PEX26 double ring D1-D2 domains with a portion of PEX5 inserted in the central pore, generated by <a href="https://github.com/google-deepmind/alphafold3/tree/main">AlphaFold 3</a>.</figcaption>
</figure>

We explore conformational ensembles using [BioEmu](https://github.com/microsoft/bioemu).

<figure>
<video width="100%" controls>
  <source src="movie_pex26_bioemu.mp4" type="video/mp4">
</video>
<figcaption><strong style="color: #6464a0;">Movie 3:</strong> Conformational ensemble of PEX26 generated by <a href="https://github.com/microsoft/bioemu">BioEmu</a>.</figcaption>
</figure>

We use [GROMACS](https://www.gromacs.org) molecular dynamics simulations to study
the effect of physical conditions such as temperature on protein behavior, and to
characterize how the **PEX26-F51L** mutation affects peroxisome function and ultimately
leads to hearing loss.

<figure>
<video width="100%" controls>
  <source src="sim_screen_rec_web.mp4" type="video/mp4">
</video>
<figcaption><strong style="color: #6464a0;">Movie 4:</strong> Molecular dynamics simulation of PEX26 in water with increasing and decreasing temperature, produced with <a href="https://www.gromacs.org">GROMACS</a>. For an interactive view with Mol* explore the dynamics <a href="dynamics.html">here</a>.</figcaption>
</figure>

We [collaborate](collaborators.html) with university laboratories specializing in peroxisome biology
and genetic hearing loss. We have contributed patient-derived fibroblasts to
these collaborations and have a mouse model of the mutation.

Our long-term goal is to advance a therapeutic pathway for this condition. We hope that
our work will also contribute to the broader understanding of more severe peroxisomal diseases on the Zellweger spectrum.

## Status

Work in progress — structural models and ensemble analysis ongoing.
